name: GitHub Copilot
description: The incumbent; agent mode plus completion across VS Code and JetBrains

developer.comment: Built by GitHub, a Microsoft subsidiary.

first-release.comment: General availability June 21, 2022 (after a technical preview that opened June 2021). Agent mode and the async coding agent came later (2025).

status.comment: Actively developed and GA; the incumbent AI coding tool. Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: The agent itself is a proprietary commercial product (a usable free tier exists, but the code is closed).

specialization.comment: Broad general-purpose coding assistant across many languages and IDEs.

interface.comment: Ships primarily as an IDE extension (VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio, Neovim), with a GA Copilot CLI and a web/github.com coding-agent surface.

host-ides.comment: VS Code, JetBrains suite, Visual Studio, Neovim/Vim, plus the Copilot CLI (terminal) and the github.com cloud coding agent (browser). Also Eclipse/Xcode per the features page.

open-source.comment: Proprietary; the agent's own source is not openly licensed (the GitHub MCP server is open source, but the agent is not).

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Primarily an IDE extension; also a CLI tool and an async cloud-service coding agent running on GitHub Actions.

default-model-provider.comment: No single default; offers a picker spanning OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), and Google (Gemini) models. Checked 2026-06-02.

model-choice.comment: Built-in model picker across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Standard offering bills through GitHub/AI Credits rather than a user-supplied provider key. VS Code has an experimental 'bring your own key' path for some models, but it is not the documented mainstream flow; marked false. Checked 2026-06-02.

local-model-support.comment: Models are vendor-hosted (Azure/GCP/Anthropic); no documented Ollama/LM Studio/local-model option.

multi-file-editing.comment: Agent mode plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agent mode iterates (edit -> run -> self-heal) until a task is complete; the cloud coding agent takes an issue end-to-end and opens a PR.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Agent mode suggests and runs terminal commands / tool calls as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: Documented Model Context Protocol support in agent mode; GitHub also ships an open-source GitHub MCP server.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Tasks can be delegated to multiple agents (Copilot, Claude, OpenAI Codex) and the async coding agent fans out work in its own environment via GitHub Actions.

codebase-indexing.comment: Builds a semantic code-search index of the repository (shared across the team) for context-enriched answers.

planning-mode.comment: The coding agent breaks an issue into a task checklist before/while executing; agents 'plan, explore, and execute'. Not a separate read-only plan toggle but an explicit plan-then-execute phase.

multimodal-input.comment: Copilot Chat / agent mode accepts image input (screenshots, mockups) when using vision-capable models.

browser-computer-use.comment: The cloud coding agent runs in its own environment and can use a Playwright-based browser tool (via MCP) to verify changes; capability is configuration-dependent. Checked 2026-06-02.

operating-mode.comment: Spans the full spectrum: tab completion, chat, supervised agent mode, autonomous task completion, and the async/background cloud coding agent.

inline-completion.comment: Originated as tab-style inline code completion; remains a core feature (unlimited completions on paid plans).

background-async-execution.comment: The cloud coding agent runs detached in its own environment while the developer does other work; opens a draft PR in the background.

persistent-memory.comment: Persistent project context via repository custom instructions and Copilot Spaces (organizational knowledge), carried across sessions. Checked 2026-06-02.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: VS Code agent mode lets you review and undo/keep agent edits (per-edit accept/discard and restore); the cloud agent works on its own branch/PR so changes are isolated. Checked 2026-06-02.

git-integration.comment: Deep native git/GitHub awareness: works on branches, commits, and opens pull requests.

pr-creation.comment: The coding agent opens and updates pull requests directly on GitHub.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports .github/copilot-instructions.md, path-specific *.instructions.md, and AGENTS.md (CLI) project instruction files.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, Copilot Extensions, and custom prompt/command files.

permission-model.comment: Agent mode asks for approval before running terminal commands/tool calls; auto-approval and allow-lists are configurable. The cloud agent runs in an isolated GitHub Actions environment.

self-host-on-prem.comment: SaaS only; no on-prem/self-hosted deployment of the agent (GitHub Enterprise Cloud / models are vendor-hosted).

privacy-mode.comment: Copilot Business and Enterprise data is not used to train models; Free/Pro/Pro+ can opt out of training. Checked 2026-06-02.

enterprise-controls.comment: Enterprise plans include SSO, audit logs, usage metrics, IP indemnity, and user management; RBAC/policy management via GitHub orgs.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier plus paid subscriptions; as of June 1, 2026 all plans moved to usage-based AI Credits billing on top of the subscription. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: Copilot Free: 2,000 completions and 50 chat/agent requests per month, no credit card required.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Copilot Pro is the cheapest paid plan at $10/month (Pro+ $39, Max $100; Business/Enterprise contact-sales). Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: GA since 2022, polished, massively deployed across major IDEs; the reference incumbent.

community-size.comment: Largest install base of any AI coding tool (millions of users / enterprise adoption).

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Copilot Extensions marketplace plus a broad MCP-server ecosystem and community instruction sharing.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads .github/copilot-instructions.md (repo-wide), AGENTS.md (also CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md agent files), and path-specific *.instructions.md files. 'custom' covers GEMINI.md and the *.instructions.md convention. Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: Organization/enterprise instructions (enterprise), personal $HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md and GitHub.com personal instructions (user), repository instructions (project), and nested AGENTS.md / path-scoped *.instructions.md (folder). Checked 2026-06-02.

scoped-rules-files.comment: *.instructions.md files use an applyTo glob to scope rules by path (path-based); .github/copilot-instructions.md and root AGENTS.md are always applied (always-on). Checked 2026-06-02.

auto-memory.comment: Copilot Memory automatically captures repository-scoped insights across sessions/surfaces (on by default for Pro/Pro+ in public preview); memories can be curated, deleted, and the feature turned off, and they auto-expire after 28 days. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Supports Agent Skills — self-contained SKILL.md folders with bundled scripts/templates/resources, loaded on demand — across VS Code, the CLI, and the cloud agent. GA-announced Dec 2025. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills appear as slash commands the user can invoke explicitly, and Copilot also selects and loads a relevant skill autonomously based on its description. Checked 2026-06-02.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive three-level loading: the skill name and description stay in context, with the SKILL.md body and bundled files pulled in only when the skill is invoked. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-distribution.comment: Curated awesome-copilot marketplace (browsable, plugin install) plus git clone and manual placement of SKILL.md folders in the skills directory. Checked 2026-06-02.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Two surfaces differ: the async cloud coding agent runs in its own ephemeral GitHub Actions environment (container-grade isolation); the local VS Code/JetBrains agent mode runs with the user's full privileges and relies on approval prompts rather than a kernel sandbox (none). Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Tools can be approved per-tool; terminal commands gate per-command via chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove regex/prefix rules (e.g. allow '^git (status|diff)', deny 'rm') so command-prefix patterns apply; the cloud coding-agent firewall allowlists by network domain (per-domain). Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: The chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove map mixes allow (true) and deny (false) regex entries, and a deny match blocks auto-approval (dangerous commands like rm/del/kill/curl ship denied); unmatched commands fall back to the confirmation prompt. No documented gitignore-style nested re-override or user-ordered first-match evaluation, so deny-overrides is the highest rung. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: User authors allow/deny rules in settings.json (chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove regex map, allowList/denyList) on top of preset postures (chat.permissions.default: default/autoApprove/autopilot). No documented programmable per-action policy hook/callback that inspects each concrete action, so the engine tops out at declarative-rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No documented out-of-the-box deny pattern for secrets (.env, key files); the default auto-approve denylist targets dangerous shell commands (rm, del, kill, curl, chmod), not sensitive file reads. A user could add deny rules, but none ships specifically protecting secret files. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: Auto-approve allow rules are written to VS Code settings.json (user or workspace scope) and persist across sessions/restarts; the cloud agent's firewall allowlist additions persist as repo/org GitHub Actions variables. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: chat.tools.global.autoApprove (and the 'autopilot' permission preset) approve all tools/commands without prompts for hands-off local runs; the async cloud coding agent is autonomous by design, working unattended in its own environment and opening a PR. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: The cloud coding agent ships a firewall enabled by default that limits outbound traffic to a recommended domain allowlist (package registries, container registries, CAs); admins can extend the allowlist or disable the firewall (configurable). Applies only to the agent's Bash tool inside the GitHub Actions environment, not MCP servers; the local IDE agent has no comparable network firewall. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box, local agent mode applies file edits (reviewable/undoable) but asks for confirmation before running terminal commands and tool calls; auto-approval is opt-in. Reflects the first-run VS Code agent-mode experience. Checked 2026-06-12.
